html:
[1]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 序 - A general term for a preface or introduction, often used in classical Chinese literature.
  • 作者自序 - Means 'the author's own preface,' indicating a self-written introduction to the work.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 长安 - Refers to Chang'an, the ancient capital of China, often used in classical Chinese literature to evoke a sense of historical grandeur.
  • 酆都 - Refers to Fengdu, a place in Chinese mythology often associated with the underworld or the afterlife.
  • 两川 - Refers to the Sichuan region, historically an important area in China.
  • 青城 - Refers to Qingcheng, a mountain range in Sichuan known for its cultural and historical significance.
  • 琵琶亭 - Refers to a pavilion associated with the playing of the pipa, a traditional Chinese musical instrument.
  • 鸾鸾 - Likely refers to a mythical or legendary figure, possibly a bird or a person with a name evoking grace or beauty.
  • 凤尾草 - Refers to a type of grass with a phoenix-like tail, often used in classical Chinese literature to symbolize elegance or rarity.
  • 武平 - Refers to a place or period, possibly a historical or mythical location.
  • 琼奴 - Refers to a female character, possibly a servant or a figure of significance in the narrative.

Proper Nouns

  • 曾序 - Likely refers to a preface written by someone named 'Zeng,' though the specific identity is unclear without additional context.
  • 王英序 - Refers to a preface written by 'Wang Ying,' a historical figure or author.
  • 羅汝敬序 - Refers to a preface written by 'Luo Rujing,' another historical figure or author.

[2]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 幔亭遇仙录 - Title referring to a record or story of encountering immortals at the Manting Pavilion.
  • 胡媚娘传 - Title indicating a biography or story of Hu Meiniang, a character who may be a historical or legendary figure.
  • 洞天花烛记 - Title suggesting a story about a fairy tale or legend involving flowers and candles, possibly set in a fairyland or celestial realm.
  • 泰山御史传 - Title indicating a biography or story of a Yushi (御史), an official in ancient China, set in the context of Mount Tai.
  • 江庙泥神记 - Title that suggests a story about a clay god or deity in a temple by the river.
  • 芙蓉屏记 - Title indicating a story about a screen decorated with lotus flowers, possibly symbolizing purity or beauty.
  • 秋千会记 - Title suggesting a story about a swing festival or gathering.
  • 贾云华还魂记 - Title indicating a story about Jia Yunhua, a character who returns to life after death.
  • 曾序 - Title indicating a preface or introduction written by Zeng (曾), likely a person's name.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 瞿氏 - Refers to the Qu family, a prominent family in Hangzhou (钱塘, Qiantang) known for their literary contributions.
  • 《剪灯新话》 - A famous collection of short stories in classical Chinese, written by Lu Zhaolin (瞿佑), which became popular during the Ming dynasty.
  • 广西布政李君昌祺 - Refers to Li Changqi, a government official from Guangxi who wrote 《剪灯余话》.
  • 圣贤之大经大法 - Refers to the fundamental principles and teachings of the sages and virtuous people in Chinese philosophy, particularly Confucianism.
  • 广西 - A province in southern China, known for its rich cultural heritage and historical significance.
  • 同年之好 - Refers to a friendship or bond formed among people who passed the same imperial examination, indicating a shared academic and social status.

Proper Nouns

  • 瞿氏 - The Qu family.
  • 《剪灯新话》 - A collection of short stories.
  • 广西布政李君昌祺 - Li Changqi, a government official from Guangxi.
  • 《剪灯余话》 - A collection of short stories compiled by Li Changqi.
  • 昌祺 - Li Changqi.
  • 广西 - A province in southern China.

[3]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 永乐庚子春闰正月下浣 - Specific date in the Yongle era of the Ming dynasty, using the lunar calendar. '庚子' is the sexagenary cycle year, '春闰正月' indicates the first month of spring with an intercalary month, and '下浣' refers to the last ten days of the month.
  • 翰林侍读学士奉训大夫兼修国史 - Title indicating a high-ranking official in the imperial court, responsible for teaching and compiling historical records. '翰林' refers to the Hanlin Academy, '侍读学士' is a scholar-official, '奉训大夫' is a rank, and '兼修国史' means he also compiled national history.
  • 窃喜昌祺之博闻广见，才高识伟，而文词制作之工且丽也 - Formal expression of admiration for someone's knowledge, talent, and literary skill. '窃喜' means 'secretly delighted,' '博闻广见' means 'extensive knowledge and wide experience,' '才高识伟' means 'great talent and profound insight,' and '文词制作之工且丽' means 'literary works that are both skillful and beautiful.'
  • 某事幽昧恍惚，君子所未信 - Phrase indicating skepticism about certain matters. '幽昧恍惚' means 'mysterious and vague,' and '君子所未信' means 'things that gentlemen do not believe.'
  • 经以载道，史以纪事 - Classical statement about the purpose of the Confucian classics and history. '经以载道' means 'the classics carry the Way,' and '史以纪事' means 'history records events.'
  • 诸子焉，托词比事 - Refers to the various schools of thought in Chinese philosophy. '诸子' means 'various masters,' '托词比事' means 'to use words to compare events.'
  • 百家之说焉，以志载古昔遗事 - Refers to the diverse traditions of Chinese thought. '百家之说' means 'the sayings of a hundred schools,' and '以志载古昔遗事' means 'to record ancient and forgotten events.'
  • 丛谈、诙语、神怪之说 - Refers to various types of discourse. '丛谈' means 'miscellaneous discussions,' '诙语' means 'humorous or witty remarks,' and '神怪之说' means 'stories of the supernatural and strange.'
  • 是非得失，固有不同 - Statement about the varying merits and demerits of different ideas. '是非得失' means 'right and wrong, gains and losses,' and '固有不同' means 'there are indeed differences.'
  • 在审择之而已 - Conclusion about the importance of careful selection. '在审择之而已' means 'it lies in careful selection alone.'
  • 言之泛溢无据者置之 - Statement about discarding unfounded claims. '言之泛溢无据者' means 'those who speak excessively without evidence,' and '置之' means 'discard them.'
  • 事核而其言不诬，有关于世教者录之 - Statement about recording verified and beneficial information. '事核而其言不诬' means 'if the facts are verified and the words are not false,' and '有关于世教者录之' means 'record those that are relevant to the world and education.'
  • 余于是编，盖亦有所取也 - Conclusion about the value of the compilation. '余于是编' means 'in this collection,' '盖亦有所取也' means 'there are indeed some valuable things.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 永乐庚子 - The year 1420 in the Yongle era of the Ming dynasty.
  • 翰林 - The Hanlin Academy, an important institution for scholars and officials in the imperial court.
  • 侍读学士 - A high-ranking scholar-official responsible for teaching and advising the emperor.
  • 奉训大夫 - A rank in the imperial bureaucracy.
  • 修国史 - The task of compiling national history.
  • 李君昌祺 - A historical figure known for his literary works.
  • 《剪灯余话》 - A collection of stories and tales, often with supernatural elements.
  • 幽冥人物灵异之事 - Stories involving supernatural beings and events.
  • 君子 - A term for a virtuous person or gentleman.
  • 经 - The Confucian classics.
  • 史 - Historical records.
  • 诸子 - Various schools of thought in Chinese philosophy.
  • 百家之说 - The diverse traditions of Chinese thought.
  • 丛谈、诙语、神怪之说 - Various types of discourse, including miscellaneous discussions, humorous remarks, and supernatural stories.
  • 世教 - The world and education.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The date '永乐庚子春闰正月下浣' is complex due to the use of the lunar calendar and the specific terminology.
  • The title '翰林侍读学士奉训大夫兼修国史' is a compound phrase that needs to be broken down into its components for clarity.
  • The sentence '窃喜昌祺之博闻广见，才高识伟，而文词制作之工且丽也' is a formal expression of admiration and needs to be rendered clearly.
  • The phrase '某事幽昧恍惚，君子所未信' is a statement of skepticism and needs to be translated accurately.
  • The classical statement '经以载道，史以纪事' is about the purpose of the classics and history.
  • The phrase '诸子焉，托词比事' refers to the various schools of thought and their methods of discourse.
  • The phrase '百家之说焉，以志载古昔遗事' refers to the diverse traditions of Chinese thought and their role in recording history.
  • The list '丛谈、诙语、神怪之说' includes various types of discourse.
  • The statement '是非得失，固有不同' is about the varying merits and demerits of different ideas.
  • The conclusion '在审择之而已' emphasizes the importance of careful selection.
  • The statement '言之泛溢无据者置之' advises discarding unfounded claims.
  • The statement '事核而其言不诬，有关于世教者录之' recommends recording verified and beneficial information.
  • The repetition '是故言之泛溢无据者置之' reinforces the idea of discarding unfounded claims.
  • The repetition '事核而其言不诬，有关于世教者录之' reinforces the idea of recording verified and beneficial information.
  • The conclusion '余于是编，盖亦有所取也' highlights the value of the compilation.

Proper Nouns

  • 永乐庚子 - The year 1420 in the Yongle era of the Ming dynasty.
  • 李君昌祺 - A historical figure known for his literary works.
  • 《剪灯余话》 - A collection of stories and tales, often with supernatural elements.
  • 翰林 - The Hanlin Academy.
  • 侍读学士 - A high-ranking scholar-official.
  • 奉训大夫 - A rank in the imperial bureaucracy.
  • 修国史 - The task of compiling national history.

[4]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 其间所述 - Refers to the content of the text being discussed.
  • 唐诸王之骄淫 - Criticizes the arrogance and licentiousness of the Tang dynasty princes.
  • 谭妇之死节 - Refers to a historical or literary figure who chose to die rather than submit to dishonor.
  • 赵鸾、琼奴之守义 - Indicates that these individuals remained loyal to moral principles.
  • 使人读之，有所惩劝 - Suggests the text serves as a moral lesson.
  • 至于他篇之作，措词命意，开阖抑扬，亦多有可取者 - Praises the artistic and thematic qualities of the works.
  • 此余之所以喜也 - Expresses personal satisfaction with the works.
  • 抑岂不闻之 - Used to introduce a known fact or tradition.
  • 昔者王充之著论，叹赏于蔡邕 - Refers to a historical instance of literary appreciation.
  • 张华之博洽，称美于阮籍 - Another historical example of literary admiration.
  • 而干宝之撰记，见称于刘恢乎 - Continuing the historical examples.
  • 操觚执翰，以著述为任者 - Refers to writers and scholars.
  • 人之所难能也 - Emphasizes the rarity of such talent.
  • 古之人盖重之 - Indicates the historical importance of writing.
  • 余何敢不企慕古人，而无所取于斯耶 - Expresses the author's respect and admiration.
  • 于是诘者乃退 - Marks the end of a debate or discussion.
  • 因书以序其端 - Explains the purpose of the preface.
  • 俾世之士皆知昌祺才识之广 - Aims to inform readers about the author's talents and knowledge.
  • 而勿讶其所著之为异也 - Addresses potential skepticism about the author's works.
  • 昌祺所作之诗词甚多，此特其游戏耳 - Clarifies the nature of the author's works.
  • 初为礼部郎中，今仕为广西左布政使 - Provides biographical information about the author.
  • 盖与余为同年进士云 - Indicates a shared academic background.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 唐诸王 - Refers to the princes of the Tang dynasty, a period known for its cultural and political achievements.
  • 谭妇 - Likely refers to a historical or literary figure, possibly a woman who demonstrated extraordinary loyalty or virtue.
  • 赵鸾、琼奴 - Specific individuals mentioned in the text, likely known for their moral integrity.
  • 礼部郎中 - A high-ranking official in the Ministry of Rites, responsible for rituals and ceremonies.
  • 广西左布政使 - A provincial governor in the Qing dynasty, responsible for administrative and financial matters.

Proper Nouns

  • 唐诸王 - The princes of the Tang dynasty.
  • 谭妇 - A historical or literary figure.
  • 赵鸾、琼奴 - Specific individuals mentioned in the text.
  • 王充 - A Han dynasty scholar and critic.
  • 蔡邕 - A Han dynasty literary figure.
  • 张华 - A Jin dynasty scholar and encyclopedist.
  • 阮籍 - A Jin dynasty poet and philosopher.
  • 干宝 - An Eastern Jin dynasty historian and mythographer.
  • 刘恢 - An Eastern Jin dynasty literary critic.
  • 礼部郎中 - A high-ranking official in the Ministry of Rites.
  • 广西左布政使 - A provincial governor in the Qing dynasty.
  • 昌祺 - The author of the works being discussed.

[5]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 凡四卷，計二十篇 - Literally 'consists of four volumes, totaling twenty chapters,' indicating the structure of the work.
  • 雄辩博洽 - Means 'eloquent and well-versed,' describing the author's qualities.
  • 昭诸翰墨 - Literally 'made known through writing,' indicating that the author's work is recorded in written form.
  • 广心志，扩见闻 - Means 'to broaden one's heart and mind, and expand one's knowledge,' indicating the educational purpose of the work.
  • 资益学识 - Means 'to enrich one's learning and knowledge,' emphasizing the intellectual value of the work.
  • 搜奇剔异 - Means 'to search for and highlight the strange and unusual,' indicating the content of the work.
  • 详书而备录之 - Means 'to write in detail and record comprehensively,' emphasizing the thoroughness of the work.
  • 有裨于时 - Means 'beneficial to the times,' indicating the practical value of these works.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 《剪灯余话》 - A collection of short stories from the Ming dynasty, known for its supernatural and romantic themes.
  • 明经擢高第 - Refers to the imperial examination system in China, where candidates were tested on Confucian classics and other subjects.
  • 名进士纂修中秘书 - Indicates a high-ranking position in the imperial bureaucracy, where scholars were responsible for compiling and editing official documents.
  • 吾儒所未信 - Reflects the skepticism of Confucian scholars towards supernatural elements, which are not part of their core beliefs.
  • 夫圣经贤传之垂宪立范 - Refers to the authoritative texts of Confucianism, which are considered the foundation of Chinese culture and morality.
  • 稗官、小说、卜筮、农圃 - Represents a diverse range of literary and practical genres, reflecting the broad scope of Chinese literature.
  • 捭阖笼罩，纵横术数之书 - Refers to books on strategy, diplomacy, and divination, which were important for governance and personal development.

Proper Nouns

  • 罗汝敬 - The author of the preface.
  • 《剪灯余话》 - The title of the work.
  • 昌祺李公 - The author of the work, Mr. Li Changqi.
  • 钱塘瞿氏 - The previous compiler of the work, Qiu from Qiantang.
  • 广西布政使 - A high-ranking official position in the Ming dynasty.
  • 明经 - A type of imperial examination.
  • 名进士 - A prestigious scholarly position.
  • 中秘书 - Central secret archives, a prestigious scholarly position.
  • 圣经贤传 - Sacred scriptures and the teachings of the wise.
  • 稗官 - Minor officials.
  • 小说 - Short stories.
  • 卜筮 - Divination.
  • 农圃 - Agriculture.
  • 捭阖笼罩 - Strategy and diplomacy.
  • 纵横术数之书 - Books on strategy, diplomacy, and divination.

[6]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 矧兹所记 - Literary phrase meaning 'let alone what is recorded here,' used to emphasize the importance of the content being discussed.
  • 贞 - Refers to chastity or fidelity, often in the context of women.
  • 节 - Refers to moral integrity or propriety, often in the context of women.
  • 廉介 - Means 'honest and upright,' describing a person's moral character.
  • 交谊 - Refers to friendship or camaraderie.
  • 雅操 - Means 'elegant conduct' or 'refined behavior.'
  • 俊杰识时 - Means 'excellent and wise individuals who understand the times.'
  • 征诸事则有验 - Means 'verified by events or facts.'
  • 揆诸理则不诬 - Means 'consistent with reason and not false.'
  • 人人所乐道 - Means 'what everyone enjoys talking about.'
  • 吾党所喜闻 - Means 'what our group or community enjoys hearing.'
  • 神异云乎哉 - Rhetorical question meaning 'is it a matter of the supernatural?'
  • 唯唯 - Expression of agreement or assent.
  • 次第之于简末 - Means 'to arrange it at the end of the scroll.'
  • 庶资薇垣高议之一噱焉 - Means 'perhaps to provide a laugh for the high-minded discussions of the officials.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 布政公 - Refers to a local official responsible for administration and governance.
  • 搜神 - Refers to the *Soushen Ji* (Records of the Supernatural), a collection of tales about gods, spirits, and supernatural phenomena.
  • 夷坚 - Refers to the *Yijian Zhi* (Records of the Unusual), another collection of strange stories.
  • 昌黎韩公 - Refers to Han Yu, a prominent scholar and writer from the Tang Dynasty, known for his literary works and philosophical writings.
  • 毛颖 - Refers to a famous pen (毛笔) in Han Yu's *Mao Ying* (The Biography of Mao Ying).
  • 革华 - Refers to a type of brush or pen, also mentioned in Han Yu's *Ge Hua* (The Biography of Ge Hua).
  • 珍果中之查梨 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'among the precious fruits, the pear and the apple,' used to describe something that is valuable but not the most exceptional.

Proper Nouns

  • 布政公 - Local official.
  • 齐谐 - The *Qi Hua*.
  • 幽冥 - The underworld or afterlife.
  • 搜神 - The *Soushen Ji*.
  • 夷坚 - The *Yijian Zhi*.
  • 昌黎韩公 - Han Yu.
  • 毛颖 - Famous pen.
  • 革华 - Type of brush or pen.
  • 珍果中之查梨 - Metaphorical expression.

[7]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 永乐十八年正月朔吉 - Formal date expression meaning 'in the first month of the year of Yongle's eighteenth year, on the first day of the month, auspicious.'
  • 翰林修撰行在工部右侍郎同年友罗汝敬书 - Formal introduction of the author, meaning 'Huanlin Xiu Zhuan, Right Minister of the Ministry of Works at the Imperial Residence, friend of the same year, Luo Rujing, writes.'
  • 爱其才思俊逸，意婉词工 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'admired his talent and thoughts as elegant and graceful, his meaning gentle and his words skillful.'
  • 锐欲效颦 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'eagerly wanted to imitate,' referring to the story of the ugly woman who tried to imitate the beautiful lady Xi Shi's frown.
  • 捃摭闻，次为二十篇 - Phrase meaning 'gathered and collected hearsay, arranged into twenty pieces,' referring to the organization of the work.
  • 仍取《还魂记》续于篇末 - Phrase meaning 'still took *The Return of the Soul* and continued it at the end of the piece,' referring to the inclusion of a previous work as an appendix.
  • 羁旅 - Term meaning 'traveling away from home,' often used to describe someone who is far from their native place.
  • 无书籍质证 - Phrase meaning 'without books to verify,' indicating a lack of references or sources to confirm the accuracy of the work.
  • 虑多抵牾 - Phrase meaning 'feared many contradictions,' indicating concern about inconsistencies in the work.
  • 寓顺城门客舍 - Phrase meaning 'stayed at a guesthouse near Shuncheng Gate,' referring to a specific location in the capital.
  • 学士曾公子棨过余 - Phrase meaning 'Scholar Zeng Gongzi Qi visited me,' referring to a scholarly visit.
  • 抚掌曰 - Phrase meaning 'clapped his hands and said,' indicating a gesture of approval or enthusiasm.
  • 兹所谓以文为戏者非耶 - Phrase meaning 'Is this not what is called writing for amusement?' indicating a question about the nature of the work.
  • 冠以叙 - Phrase meaning 'prefaced with a preface,' referring to the addition of an introductory section.
  • 称其秾丽丰蔚，文采烂然 - Phrase meaning 'praised its rich and lush beauty, its splendid literary style,' indicating a high level of literary praise.
  • 稍稍人知 - Phrase meaning 'gradually people began to know,' indicating a growing awareness of the work.
  • 竞求抄录 - Phrase meaning 'rushed to copy it,' indicating a high demand for the work.
  • 亟欲焚去以绝迹 - Phrase meaning 'eagerly wanted to burn it to eliminate traces,' indicating a desire to destroy the work.
  • 索者踵至 - Phrase meaning 'those who asked for it came one after another,' indicating a continuous demand for the work.
  • 势不容拒矣 - Phrase meaning 'the situation did not allow refusal,' indicating that the author could not refuse requests to share the work.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 永乐十八年 - The eighteenth year of the Yongle Emperor's reign (1420), a significant period in Chinese history when the capital was moved from Nanjing to Beijing.
  • 翰林修撰 - A high-ranking scholar-official in the Hanlin Academy, a prestigious institution in the imperial court.
  • 行在工部右侍郎 - A high-ranking official in the Ministry of Works at the Imperial Residence, indicating a position of significant influence.
  • 同年友 - A term of friendship among scholars who passed the imperial examinations in the same year.
  • 罗汝敬 - The name of the author, indicating a scholar-official of the Ming dynasty.
  • 《柔柔传》 - A work by Guibin Gui, likely a literary piece that the author admired.
  • 《还魂记》 - A work that the author had previously written, possibly a play or narrative.
  • 《剪灯新话》 - A collection of short stories by Qu Yuan, a Ming dynasty writer known for his romantic tales.
  • 房山 - A place name, likely referring to a specific location in Beijing where the author was working.
  • 羁旅 - A term often used in classical Chinese to describe someone who is far from home, often in a state of travel or exile.
  • 学士曾公子棨 - A scholar who visited the author, indicating a social and intellectual connection.

Proper Nouns

  • 永乐十八年 - The eighteenth year of the Yongle Emperor's reign.
  • 翰林修撰 - A high-ranking scholar-official in the Hanlin Academy.
  • 行在工部右侍郎 - A high-ranking official in the Ministry of Works at the Imperial Residence.
  • 同年友 - A term of friendship among scholars who passed the imperial examinations in the same year.
  • 罗汝敬 - The name of the author.
  • 《柔柔传》 - A work by Guibin Gui.
  • 《还魂记》 - A work by the author.
  • 《剪灯新话》 - A collection of short stories by Qu Yuan.
  • 房山 - A place name.
  • 学士曾公子棨 - A scholar who visited the author.

[8]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 饱食终日，无所用心 - Proverb meaning 'to eat one's fill all day long without using one's mind,' criticizing idleness.
  • 不有博弈者乎？为之犹贤乎已！ - Rhetorical question meaning 'Isn't there something like playing games? Doing that is still better than nothing!' Suggests that even leisure activities are preferable to complete inactivity.
  • 豁怀抱，宣郁闷 - 'To open up one's heart and express one's inner frustrations.' Describes the act of venting emotions.
  • 虽知其近于滑稽谐谑，而不遑恤者 - Although I know that this is close to being humorous and playful, but I cannot worry about that.
  • 千载之下，脍炙人口 - Even a thousand years later, they are still widely praised.
  • 俾时自省览，以毋忘前日之虞，而保其终吉 - So that I can reflect on it from time to time, to never forget the worries of the past, and to ensure a good ending.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 高唐 - Title of a famous poem from the *Chuci* (Songs of Chu).
  • 洛神 - Title of a poem by Cao Zhi.
  • 永乐 - Reign title of Emperor Yongle of the Ming Dynasty (1402–1424).
  • 庐陵 - Ancient name for the area around modern-day Nanchang, Jiangxi Province.
  • 李祯昌祺甫 - Author's name and courtesy name. '甫' (fǔ) is a common courtesy name suffix.

Proper Nouns

  • 高唐 - Title of a famous poem from the *Chuci*.
  • 洛神 - Title of a poem by Cao Zhi.
  • 永乐 - Reign title of Emperor Yongle.
  • 庐陵 - Ancient name for the area around modern-day Nanchang, Jiangxi Province.
  • 李祯昌祺甫 - Author's name and courtesy name.

[9]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 洪武初 - Refers to the early years of the Hongwu era (1368–1398), the reign of the first Ming emperor, Zhu Yuanzhang.
  • 老成练达 - Means 'experienced and well-versed,' describing someone who is mature and knowledgeable.
  • 政事文章 - Refers to both governance and literary accomplishments, often used together to denote a person’s overall competence.
  • 推重当代 - Means 'highly esteemed in the present age,' indicating widespread respect and admiration.
  • 秦邸之国 - Refers to the establishment of the Prince of Qin’s fiefdom, a significant political event in the early Ming dynasty.
  • 右辅 - Title given to a high-ranking official, typically the Chancellor.
  • 左辅 - Title given to a high-ranking official, typically the Vice-Chancellor.
  • 导翊 - Means 'guidance and support,' referring to the role of these officials in assisting the prince.
  • 登高能赋 - A classical allusion to the ability to compose poetry while enjoying the scenery, often associated with the talent of Li Bai.
  • 案牍之劳 - Refers to the tedious work of official paperwork, a common theme in classical literature.
  • 休退之日 - Means 'retirement day,' referring to the future when the officials might step down from their positions.
  • 临眺于山川 - Means 'to gaze upon mountains and rivers,' a common theme in classical Chinese literature, often associated with contemplation and appreciation of nature.
  • 访古寻幽 - Means 'to visit ancient sites and explore secluded places,' reflecting a desire to connect with the past and nature.
  • 随从以行 - Means 'to accompany on the journey,' indicating the close relationship between the officials and the prince.
  • 未尝相舍 - Means 'they never parted,' emphasizing the close friendship between the two officials.
  • 高十二丈，百二十七步 - Describes the height and dimensions of the mausoleums, using traditional Chinese units of measurement.
  • 形方正，状类覆斗 - Means 'square and upright, resembling an overturned斗,' describing the shape of the mausoleum.
  • 陵东为卫将军青墓 - Refers to the tomb of Wei Qing, a famous general during the Han dynasty.
  • 稍东为霍去病墓 - Refers to the tomb of Huo Qubing, another famous general, known for his military campaigns against the Xiongnu.
  • 所谓象祁连山者 - Means 'as it is said, resembling Mount Qilian,' describing the shape of Huo Qubing’s tomb.
  • 西北，为公孙弘墓 - Refers to the tomb of Gong Sun Hong, a prominent statesman and scholar.
  • 西一里为李夫人墓 - Refers to the tomb of Lady Li, a concubine of Emperor Wu of Han.
  • 山川雄秀 - Means 'majestic and beautiful mountains and rivers,' describing the natural landscape.
  • 与他处异 - Means 'different from other places,' emphasizing the unique beauty of the area.
  • 宜先于是 - Means 'it is best to start here,' suggesting that this location should be visited first.
  • 兴平去此十八里 - Means 'Xingping is eighteen li away,' indicating the distance from the current location to Xingping.
  • 一日可到 - Means 'it can be reached in one day,' suggesting the feasibility of the journey.
  • 暨归，至半途，期仁马乏，追公不及 - Means 'upon returning, halfway, the horse of Qi Ren was exhausted, and he could not catch up,' describing the situation where Qi Ren fell behind.
  • 因缓辔徐行，不觉暝矣 - Means 'so he slowed down and walked slowly, unaware that it had become dark,' describing the peaceful and gradual transition into evening.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 天黑，将近二更 - Colloquial expression indicating it is getting dark and approaching midnight.
  • 禽鸟飞鸣，狐兔充斥 - Describes the scene of birds flying and calling, and foxes and rabbits being abundant, indicating a wild and eerie environment.
  • 心甚恐，且畏且行 - Phrase expressing fear and hesitation while moving forward.
  • 俄而望中隐隐有火光 - Indicates that suddenly, there is a faint light in the distance.
  • 意谓人家不远 - Suggests that the traveler believes a human settlement is nearby.
  • 策马以进 - Means to urge the horse forward.
  • 至则果民舍也 - Indicates that upon arrival, it is indeed a civilian house.
  • 双户洞开，灯犹未灭 - Describes the house with its doors wide open and lights still burning.
  • 期仁下马，拴于庭树之上 - Means the traveler dismounts and ties the horse to a tree in the courtyard.
  • 入坐客次 - Indicates entering and sitting in a guest area.
  • 良久寂然，不敢叩门 - Describes a long period of silence, with the traveler hesitating to knock on the door.
  • 惟屡謦咳 - Means to cough repeatedly to make oneself known.
  • 使其家知之 - Indicates the intention to make the household aware of the traveler's presence.
  • 少顷，苍头自便户出 - Describes a short time later, a servant (苍头) appears from the side door.
  • 问客何来 - Means the servant asks where the guest has come from.
  • 期仁以实告 - Indicates the traveler tells the truth.
  • 苍头唯唯而去 - Describes the servant nodding and leaving.
  • 未几，主人出 - Indicates shortly after, the host appears.
  • 乃一少年，韦布翛然，状貌温粹 - Describes the host as a young man dressed casually and with a gentle and refined appearance.
  • 揖客与语，言辞简当 - Means the host greets the guest and speaks in simple and appropriate terms.
  • 问劳而已 - Indicates the conversation is limited to greetings and inquiries about the guest's well-being.
  • 茶罢，延入中堂 - Describes the host inviting the guest into the main hall after tea.
  • 规制幽雅可爱 - Means the layout is elegant and charming.
  • 花卉芬芳，几席雅洁 - Describes the flowers as fragrant and the tables and seats as clean and elegant.
  • 坐定，少年呼其妻出拜 - Indicates the young man calls his wife to greet the guest.
  • 视之，国色也 - Describes the wife as exceptionally beautiful.
  • 年二十余，靓妆常服，不屑朱铅 - Means she is in her twenties, dressed in everyday attire, and does not use makeup.
  • 往来于香烟烛影中，绰约若仙姝神女 - Describes her graceful movements in the light of incense and candles, resembling a fairy or goddess.
  • 期仁私念彼寻常人，而妻美若此，必怪也 - Indicates the traveler thinks it strange that an ordinary person could have such a beautiful wife.
  • 亦不敢问 - Means the traveler is too shy to ask.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The historian's failure to record the names creates a gap in the narrative.
  • This clause introduces a secondary source, which may not be entirely reliable.
  • This sentence sets up the initial event, but the timeline is ambiguous.
  • The prince's actions and the woman's reaction are described in a sequence that may be interpreted differently.
  • This clause clarifies the actual duration, contrasting it with the reported version.
  • The woman's actions and the prince's response are described in a way that highlights the discrepancy.
  • This clause explicitly denies the prince's involvement in certain actions.
  • This rhetorical question emphasizes the severity of the slander.
  • This clause introduces a secondary literary work based on the story.
  • This clause criticizes the exaggeration in the literary work.
  • This clause quotes a specific line from the literary work, highlighting the contrast between the luxurious and humble dwellings.

Proper Nouns

  • 唐宁王 - Prince Ning of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 《本事集》 - *The Collection of True Events*.
  • 金屋 - Golden house.
  • 茆檐 - Thatched roof.
  • 骚人墨客 - Poets and literati.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 呜呼！ - An interjection expressing sorrow or lamentation, often used at the beginning of a passage.
  • 回思尔时 - Literally means 'reflecting back on that time,' used to introduce a reminiscence.
  • 事出迫夺 - Means 'the matter arose from coercion or force,' indicating that the situation was forced upon someone.
  • 薰天之势 - A metaphor for an overwhelming or tyrannical power, often used to describe a powerful and oppressive force.
  • 妾夫尚敢喘息耶？ - A rhetorical question meaning 'Could my husband dare to breathe?' implying that he was in a position of extreme danger or oppression.
  • 轻一诺为妾夫罪 - Means 'to be faulted for my husband's light promise,' suggesting that he was blamed for a trivial action.
  • 岂不冤哉？ - A rhetorical question meaning 'Is it not unjust?' expressing a sense of injustice.
  • 有恳托公者，此也 - Means 'the matter I entrust to you is this,' indicating the subject of the request.
  • 期仁曰 - 'Period Ren said,' where '期仁' is a name or title.
  • 若尔守义，实为可嘉 - Means 'If you adhere to your principles, it is truly commendable.'
  • 正须直笔，以励风俗 - Means 'It is necessary to write honestly to encourage good customs.'
  • 使之昧昧无闻 - Means 'to let it remain unknown or unacknowledged.'
  • 安得不饮恨于九原，抱痛于百世哉？ - A rhetorical question meaning 'How could one not drink hatred in the underworld and bear pain for a hundred generations?' expressing deep regret and sorrow.
  • 期仁不敏 - Means 'Period Ren is not wise,' a humble self-assessment.
  • 滥以文辞称 - Means 'to be praised for my writing,' indicating modesty about one's literary abilities.
  • 当为子表而出之 - Means 'I will write it out for you,' indicating a commitment to action.
  • 胶于见闻 - Means 'to be stuck in one's perceptions or experiences,' suggesting a lack of flexibility or openness to new ideas.
  • 一旦厘正，不免人疑 - Means 'If corrected at once, people will inevitably doubt,' indicating concern about potential skepticism.
  • 愿得子姓字 - Means 'I wish to know your name,' indicating a request for identification.
  • 以补史氏之缺 - Means 'to fill the gap in historical records,' indicating a desire to correct or complete historical documentation.
  • 可乎？ - A polite question meaning 'Is it acceptable?' or 'Is it permissible?'
  • 少年愀然不乐 - Means 'The young man looked solemn and unhappy,' indicating a change in mood.
  • 若显余姓名人间 - Means 'If my name is revealed to the world,' indicating a concern about exposure.
  • 负愧愈无尽矣 - Means 'The guilt and shame will be endless,' indicating a sense of overwhelming responsibility or regret.
  • 非所愿也 - Means 'It is not what I desire,' indicating a clear preference.
  • 乞以前所云者，辩正足矣 - Means 'I ask that the previous statement be corrected,' indicating a request for clarification or correction.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 呜呼！ - A common expression in classical Chinese literature, often used to express deep sorrow or lamentation.
  • 妾夫 - Refers to the speaker's husband, indicating a marital relationship.
  • 薰天之势 - A metaphor for a powerful and oppressive force, often used in historical and literary contexts to describe tyrannical rulers or overwhelming circumstances.
  • 九原 - A term for the underworld or the place where the dead reside, often used in classical literature to express deep sorrow or regret.
  • 期仁 - A name or title, possibly referring to a scholar or official.
  • 史氏 - Refers to historians or historical records, indicating a concern with historical accuracy and documentation.

Proper Nouns

  • 期仁 - A name or title, possibly referring to a scholar or official.
  • 史氏 - Refers to historians or historical records.

  • 作《贞松操》，写之丝桐 - Composed the 'Song of the Chaste Pine' and wrote it on the qin.
  • 忽有美姬自外入 - Suddenly, a beautiful maiden entered from outside.
  • 妾姓钟，名碧桃，宋谭节妇侍儿也 - I am a maiden named Zhōng Bìtáo, a servant of the chaste wife of Song Tán.
  • 主母贞节，上帝嘉之，已位高仙 - The lady of the house is chaste and virtuous, praised by the Heavenly Deity, and has already attained a high rank among the immortals.
  • 见莅南岳左右魏夫人所，享天上之乐矣 - She presides over the place of Lady Wei in the vicinity of Mount Heng and enjoys the music of heaven.
  • 太上以其影留下界，恐人亵慢，将命六丁取之 - The highest deity, fearing disrespect, ordered the Six Dings to retrieve her shadow.
  • 使之衣服冠而坐，藏诸洞天 - Dressed her in clothes and a hat and had her sit, hiding her in the celestial realms.
  • 文昌忠孝司言：影在孔子礼殿，托得其所 - The office of Virtue and Filial Piety stated that the shadow is in Confucius' ritual hall, placed in its proper place.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 节妇 - A woman who remains faithful to her husband even after his death, a concept deeply rooted in Confucian ethics.
  • 贞松操 - A musical composition that symbolizes chastity and steadfastness, often used in classical Chinese literature to represent moral integrity.
  • 丝桐 - A poetic term for the qin, a traditional Chinese zither, often associated with scholars and intellectuals.
  • 南岳 - Mount Heng in Hunan Province, one of the Five Great Mountains of China, often associated with Daoist and Buddhist traditions.
  • 魏夫人 - Lady Wei, a prominent figure in Daoist mythology, often associated with the goddess of the South.
  • 六丁 - The Six Dings, a group of Daoist deities often involved in protective and magical tasks.
  • 文昌忠孝司 - The office of Virtue and Filial Piety, a Daoist institution responsible for rewarding virtuous individuals.
  • 孔子礼殿 - Confucius' ritual hall, a place of great cultural and religious significance in Chinese tradition.

  • 缘妾前世为女医，误投人药，致损贵胎，以故再世罚为女身以偿，坐此少缓，尚隔两尘 - Means 'because in my previous life I was a female physician, I mistakenly administered medicine, causing harm to a noble fetus, and thus in this life I am punished to be reborn as a woman to atone, and because of this, I am slightly delayed, still separated by two layers of existence,' explaining a karmic explanation for a delay.
  • 然则汝亦良家子乎 - Means 'then are you also from a good family?' asking about someone's background.
  • 父母以贫故，鬻于赵氏 - Means 'my parents sold me to the Zhao family because of poverty,' describing a transaction due to economic hardship.
  • 赵，故宋宗室也，售妾以媵其女 - Means 'Zhao, a former member of the Song royal family, bought me to serve as a concubine for his daughter,' providing context for the transaction.
  • 女即节妇，与妾年相若，蒙其怜爱，视犹骨肉 - Means 'the girl is the chaste woman, and she is about the same age as I, she showed me kindness and affection, treating me like family,' describing a relationship.
  • 及归谭氏，妾从行焉 - Means 'when she married into the Tan family, I accompanied her,' indicating a shared journey.
  • 时谭方门庭鼎盛，圭组蝉联 - Means 'at that time, the Tan family was at the height of its prosperity, with officials and nobles in succession,' describing the family's status.
  • 褥隐绣芙蓉，极一时之富贵 - Means 'the bed was adorned with embroidered peonies, representing the utmost wealth and splendor of the time,' describing luxury.
  • 砚寒金井水，洒万斛之珠玑 - Means 'the inkstone was cooled with water from the golden well, and it was sprinkled with countless pearls and jewels,' describing opulence.

  • 妾侍左右，饱闻训言；虽在贱微，颇习诗礼 - Indicates that the narrator, being in service, has heard much of the master's teachings and has learned much of poetry and propriety despite their lowly status.

  • 我主母洁身就死，而婢子忍耻偷生，颠沛流离，窜伏林莽 - Describes the main lady's honorable death and the narrator's own survival through hardship and hiding in the wilderness.
  • 主恩难报，徒怀结草之心；女质易殂，竟作翳桑之鬼 - Expresses the narrator's inability to repay the master's kindness and their own frailty, leading to an early demise.
  • 物情恶衰歇，谁招碧玉之游魂；吾道属艰难，畴葬绿珠之弱骨 - Laments the world's aversion to decline and the difficulty of the path, questioning who will remember the deceased.

  • 劍逐驚波玉委塵 - A metaphorical phrase where '劍逐驚波' means 'the sword chases the startled waves,' and '玉委塵' means 'the jade falls to dust.' This suggests a scene of dramatic action followed by the loss or degradation of something precious.
  • 青血化為原上草 - A metaphorical expression where '青血' means 'green blood' (likely referring to fresh blood), and '化為原上草' means 'transforms into grass on the plain.' This suggests a transformation from something precious (blood) into something common (grass).
  • 人生莫作婦人身 - A proverbial saying meaning 'one should not be born a woman,' reflecting historical gender biases.
  • 百年世事不勝悲 - A poetic expression meaning 'a hundred years of worldly affairs are unbearable to bear,' expressing deep sorrow over the passage of time and the vicissitudes of life.
  • 大厦原非一木支 - A metaphorical phrase meaning 'a great building is not supported by a single piece of wood,' emphasizing the importance of collective effort.
  • 慷慨西风泪横臆 - A poetic expression where '慷慨' means 'generous' or 'heroic,' '西风' means 'western wind' (often associated with autumn), and '泪横臆' means 'tears streaming across the chest.' This suggests a scene of heroic emotion in the face of adversity.
  • 此心惟有老天知 - A poetic expression meaning 'only the old heaven knows this heart,' expressing deep personal sorrow or frustration.
  • 血迸金枪卧铁衣 - A metaphorical phrase where '血迸金枪' means 'blood spurts from the golden spear,' and '卧铁衣' means 'lying in iron armor.' This suggests a scene of intense combat.
  • 江山犹是昔人非 - A poetic expression meaning 'the mountains and rivers remain, but the people have changed,' reflecting the impermanence of human life.
  • 旧时王谢堂前燕 - A well-known allusion to the decline of noble families, where '王谢' refers to the Wang and Xie families, and '堂前燕' means 'swallows in the hall.' This suggests the passage of time and the fall of once-great families.
  • 更傍谁家门户飞 - A poetic expression meaning 'now flying to whose door?' suggesting a sense of loss and uncertainty.

  • 莫怨春风当自嗟 - A poetic expression meaning 'do not blame the spring wind, but lament yourself,' suggesting self-reflection and acceptance.
  • 侧垂高髻插金钿 - A poetic expression describing a woman's hairstyle, where '侧垂高髻' means 'a high bun tilted to one side,' and '插金钿' means 'adorned with gold and jade ornaments.'
  • 闲过春风六六年 - A poetic expression meaning 'passing idly through six or seven springs,' suggesting the passage of time.

  • 以性學自任 - Means he took upon himself the study of human nature or moral philosophy.
  • 酷不喜老、佛 - Strongly dislikes Daoism and Buddhism.
  • 間遇其徒於道 - Means encountering their followers on the road.
  • 輒斥之曰 - Always rebukes them by saying...
  • 四民之中 - Refers to the four classes of people: scholars, farmers, artisans, and merchants.
  • 縱不為士 - Even if not being a scholar.
  • 為農、為工、商 - Being a farmer, artisan, or merchant.
  • 何至為是哉 - Why would they resort to such things?
  • 著《警論》三篇 - Wrote three essays titled *Jing Lun* (Warning Discourse).
  • 每篇反復數千言 - Each essay is thousands of words long, emphasizing repetition and elaboration.
  • 推明天理 - Elaborates on the principles of heaven and reason.
  • 辨析異端 - Analyzes and refutes heterodox teachings.
  • 匡正人心 - Corrects and guides people's hearts.
  • 扶植世教 - Supports and upholds the world's moral teachings.
  • 上篇略曰 - The first essay briefly states...
  • 先儒謂 - Ancient scholars said...
  • 天即理也 - Heaven is the principle.
  • 以其形體而言，謂之天 - When speaking of its physical form, it is called heaven.
  • 以其主宰而言，謂之帝 - When speaking of its sovereign role, it is called the emperor.
  • 帝即天，天即帝 - The emperor is heaven, and heaven is the emperor.
  • 非蒼蒼之上，別有一天 - It is not another heaven above the blue sky.
  • 宮室居處，端冕垂旒 - Refers to the palaces and regalia of the emperor, symbolizing divine authority.
  • 若世之帝王者 - Like the emperors and kings of the world.
  • 此釋、老之論也 - This is the view of Buddhism and Daoism.
  • 不特此也 - Not only this, but...
  • 又有所謂三天、九天、三十三天 - Also refers to the three heavens, nine heavens, and thirty-three heavens.
  • 三帝、九帝、十方諸帝 - Three emperors, nine emperors, and emperors in all directions.
  • 何天之多而帝之眾耶 - Why so many heavens and so many emperors?
  • 由是言之 - Therefore...
  • 天未免如階級之形 - Heaven is not free from the appearance of a hierarchy.
  • 帝未免有割據之争矣 - Emperors are not free from territorial disputes.
  • 甚者尊漢張道陵為天師 - Some even regard Zhang Daoling of Han as the Heavenly Master.
  • 天豈有師乎 - How can heaven have a teacher?
  • 以宋林氏女為天妃 - Regard Lin, a woman from Song, as the Heavenly Consort.
  • 天果有妃乎 - Does heaven really have a consort?
  • 蓋天者，理之所從出 - Heaven is the source of principles.
  • 聖人法天 - Sages follow the example of heaven.
  • 道陵縱聖，亦人鬼耳 - Even if Zhang Daoling is a sage, he is still a human ghost.
  • 使天而師之，是天乃道陵之不若也 - If heaven were to regard him as a teacher, it would mean that heaven is inferior to Zhang Daoling.
  • 林女既死，特游魂耳 - Lin, after death, is merely a wandering spirit.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 大宋 - Refers to the Song Dynasty (960–1279 CE), a period of significant cultural and intellectual development in China.
  • 五經 - The Five Classics: *Shijing* (Book of Poetry), *Shujing* (Book of Documents), *Yijing* (Book of Changes), *Liji* (Book of Rites), and *Chunqiu* (Spring and Autumn Annals). These are foundational texts in Confucianism.
  • 《易》 - The *Yijing* (Book of Changes), one of the Five Classics, dealing with divination and philosophical concepts.
  • 性學 - The study of human nature, a key theme in Confucian philosophy.
  • 老、佛 - Refers to Daoism (Laozi) and Buddhism, two major philosophical and religious traditions in China.
  • 四民 - The four classes of society: scholars, farmers, artisans, and merchants, a concept rooted in Confucian social hierarchy.
  • 《警論》 - The title of the essays written by He Siming, which aim to warn and educate.
  • 先儒 - Ancient scholars or Confucian masters who interpreted and expounded the classics.
  • 三天、九天、三十三天 - Concepts from Daoist cosmology, referring to different levels of heaven.
  • 三帝、九帝、十方諸帝 - Ideas from Buddhist cosmology, referring to various deities or rulers in different realms.
  • 漢張道陵 - Zhang Daoling (34–156 CE), a Daoist figure who founded the Way of the Celestial Masters (Tianshi Dao).
  • 宋林氏女 - A reference to Lin Miaoshan, a Song Dynasty woman who was venerated as a deity in some folk traditions.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

Cultural and Historical Allusions

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '始吾病革时，见两苍蝇堕床前，视之，已变为人矣' is elliptical, describing a sudden transformation of flies into human beings. The phrase '视之，已变为人矣' can be read as a continuation of the previous action, but the exact timing of the transformation is ambiguous.
  • The sentence '余坐袋内，两人持之行树巅如飞' describes the scholar being carried through the air, but the exact mechanics of the movement are unclear. The phrase '行树巅如飞' suggests flying, but it could also mean moving swiftly through the trees.
  • The sentence '又半日，方有路，始出余袋中' describes a long journey before the scholar is finally released from the bag. The phrase '方有路' suggests that the path became visible after a long time, but the exact meaning is ambiguous.
  • The sentence '押过一所，若把截处，守者高鼻深目，拳发胡须，类回回人' describes a checkpoint or barrier, but the exact nature of the '守者' (guards) and their role is unclear. The phrase '若把截处' suggests a barrier or checkpoint, but the exact meaning is ambiguous.

  • 门人 - Disciples or students.
  • 子 - A respectful term for a teacher or sage, often used to refer to Confucius.

  • 网罟 - A type of fishing net.
  • 回回人 - A term used in historical Chinese texts to refer to Central Asian or Persian people.
  • 朱篆 - A red seal, likely indicating an official or divine authority.

  • 帝至尊 - The emperor is supreme, emphasizing the highest authority.
  • 以割据戏之 - Play with the idea of division and rule, suggesting disrespectful behavior towards the emperor.
  • 妄论天师之号 - Unjustly discuss the title of Celestial Master, indicating inappropriate discussion of religious titles.
  • 妄辨天妃之称 - Unjustly debate the title of Heavenly Consort, indicating inappropriate discussion of religious titles.
  • 其罪大矣 - His sin is great, indicating severe consequences for the actions described.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 黄巾 - Refers to the Yellow Turban Rebellion, a significant peasant uprising during the late Eastern Han dynasty.
  • 台尊 - Refers to a high-ranking official or dignitary, often used in formal settings.
  • 鸿濛 - Refers to the primordial chaos or the beginning of the universe in Chinese cosmology.
  • 圣智 - Refers to the wisdom of the sages, often associated with Confucian teachings.
  • 物理 - Refers to the principles of nature, a concept central to both Confucian and Daoist thought.
  • 辟干阖坤 - Refers to the manipulation of natural elements, a concept often found in Daoist alchemy and cosmology.
  • 橐龠 - Refers to a bellows, a tool used in metallurgy and alchemy, symbolizing the creation of harmony.
  • 阴阳动静 - Refers to the fundamental forces of yin and yang, which govern the natural world.
  • 仙真 - Refers to immortals or sages, often associated with Daoist and Buddhist traditions.
  • 道佛 - Refers to Daoism and Buddhism, two major religious traditions in China.
  • 天师 - Refers to the Celestial Master, a title associated with Daoist leaders.
  • 天妃 - Refers to the Heavenly Consort, a title associated with a female deity in Chinese mythology.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 且儒书中言天者不一 - Indicates multiple ways of discussing 'heaven' in Confucian texts.
  • 若《春秋》书‘天王’，《诗》称‘伣天之妹’、‘昊天其子’ - Specific examples of how 'heaven' is referred to in different texts.
  • 使皆若尔论 - Means 'if they were all like your argument,' suggesting a hypothetical scenario.
  • 天既无师与妃 - Translates to 'heaven has neither teacher nor consort,' a literal interpretation.
  • 又安得有王、有妹、有子者乎 - Translates to 'how could there be a king, a sister, or a son?' questioning the logic of the previous statement.
  • 尔之学诚拘而不通，滞而有碍 - Translates to 'your learning is indeed confined and not thorough, obstructed and hindered.'
  • 拘则局于一器，滞则胶于一隅 - Translates to 'confined means being limited to one tool, obstructed means being stuck in one corner.'
  • 不通则固陋，有碍则鄙僻 - Translates to 'not thorough leads to narrow-mindedness, hindered leads to narrow and biased thinking.'
  • 真俗腐迂谬之士，胡可冒儒者之名乎 - Translates to 'truly vulgar, corrupt, and absurd people, how can they claim to be Confucian scholars?'
  • 命取何姓簿来，于余姓名下，以朱笔抹之，复傍注之，毕 - Translates to 'order the retrieval of the register of the surname He, mark my name with a red pen, and then annotate it, finishing the task.'
  • 省谕曰：‘尔本合为六品官，出入华要。由尔弗信仙佛，诬罔鬼神，特降为七品。’ - Translates to 'the provincial governor said: 'You should originally be a sixth-rank official, entering and leaving important places. Because you do not believe in gods and Buddhas, and deceive ghosts and spirits, you are specially demoted to a seventh-rank official.'
  • 余顿首谢 - Translates to 'I bowed and thanked.'
  • 台尊曰：‘此人面承腹诽，退有后言，可令阅狱，折服其心。’ - Translates to 'the provincial governor said: 'This person pretends to agree but disapproves inwardly, and speaks ill of others behind their backs. He should be made to see the prison, to convince his heart.'
  • 数卒捽余下，付黄巾领去省业司 - Translates to 'several soldiers pulled me down and handed me over to a man in yellow robes to take to the provincial office.'
  • 中有宝塔一座，僧立塔傍，香烛幡幢，荧煌罗列 - Translates to 'there was a treasure pagoda, a monk standing beside it, incense, candles, banners, and streamers arranged brightly.'
  • 黄巾再拜，余亦拜 - Translates to 'the man in yellow robes bowed twice, and I also bowed.'
  • 僧开塔取一大珠，以金盘承之，黄巾以双手擎捧前行，余随之，皆幽暗境也 - Translates to 'the monk opened the pagoda and took out a large pearl, placed it in a golden tray, and the man in yellow robes held it with both hands as we proceeded, all in a dim and dark place.'
  • 余问：‘僧谁乎？’曰：‘导冥和尚也。’ - Translates to 'I asked: 'Who is the monk?' He said: 'He is the Guiding to the Underworld monk.'
  • 又问：‘珠何为？’曰：‘地藏王菩萨愿珠也。狱中业气深重，赖珠光照破。不尔，则鬼王于暗中食人心肝，不得出矣。’ - Translates to 'I asked: 'What is the purpose of the pearl?' He said: 'It is the wish-fulfilling pearl of the Bodhisattva Ksitigarbha. In the prison, the karma is heavy, and it relies on the light of the pearl to dispel it. Otherwise, the ghost king would eat the hearts and livers of people in the dark, and they would not be able to leave.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 儒书 - Refers to Confucian texts, which were foundational in Chinese culture and governance.
  • 《春秋》 - The Spring and Autumn Annals, one of the Five Classics, a historical record of the state of Lu.
  • 《诗》 - The Book of Poetry, another of the Five Classics, a collection of ancient Chinese poetry.
  • 天王 - A title used in the *Spring and Autumn Annals* to refer to the king of Zhou.
  • 伣天之妹 - A poetic expression referring to a woman as a sister of heaven, often used in the *Book of Poetry*.
  • 昊天其子 - A poetic expression referring to a person as a child of heaven, also from the *Book of Poetry*.
  • 六品官 - A sixth-rank official in the Chinese bureaucracy, indicating a relatively high position.
  • 仙佛 - Refers to immortals and Buddhas, important figures in Chinese religious and philosophical traditions.
  • 鬼神 - Refers to ghosts and spirits, often associated with folk beliefs and religious practices.
  • 地藏王菩萨 - The Bodhisattva Ksitigarbha, a significant figure in Mahayana Buddhism known for his vow to save all beings from suffering.
  • 省业司 - A provincial office responsible for various administrative functions.
  • 宝塔 - A pagoda, a type of tower used in Chinese architecture, often associated with Buddhist temples.
  • 香烛幡幢 - Incense, candles, banners, and streamers, common elements in religious rituals.
  • 荧煌罗列 - Brightly arranged, describing the arrangement of these ritual items.
  • 导冥和尚 - The Guiding to the Underworld monk, a figure who guides souls in the afterlife.
  • 地藏王菩萨愿珠 - The wish-fulfilling pearl of the Bodhisattva Ksitigarbha, a symbol of enlightenment and protection.
  • 狱中业气深重 - The heavy karma in the prison, indicating the severity of the sins or wrongdoings of the souls there.
  • 鬼王于暗中食人心肝 - The ghost king eating the hearts and livers of people in the dark, a metaphor for the punishment of the wicked in the afterlife.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 於是首造一獄 - Formal expression meaning 'then he first created a prison.'

  • 刺入人眼 - Means 'pierced into the eyes.'

  • 如懸槁魚 - Means 'like hanging dried fish.'
  • 黄巾曰 - Means 'the Yellow Turban said.'
  • 此男子在世，不能恭友兄弟，視如秦越，輕滅大倫，惟重財利，受此報也 - Means 'This man in his lifetime did not respect his brothers, treating them like distant relatives, and disrespected the great bond of kinship, valuing only wealth and profit, and thus received this punishment.'
  • 次一獄曰 - Means 'the next prison was called.'

  • 旋轉不已 - Means 'continuously spinning.'

  • 痛不可當 - Means 'the pain was unbearable.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 剔镂 - A specific form of torture, possibly derived from historical or fictional accounts of cruel punishments.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 鄰剔镂獄曰‘秽溷’狱 - Hellish realm called 'filthy latrine' (秽溷), where the condemned suffer.
  • 狱尽大粪池，滚沸如汤 - Vivid description of the hellish environment using the simile 'boiling like soup' (滚沸如汤).
  • 鬼以长叉叉人下煮之 - Demons use 'long spears' (长叉) to skewer and boil the condemned.
  • 出没其间，顷刻溃烂，化为蛆虫 - Transformation of the tortured into worms using the verb '化' (hua).
  • 又以竹箩捞蛆于锅中，细炒之，炒辄成灰 - Demons scoop and cook the maggots using 'bamboo baskets' (竹箩) and 'finely fry' (细炒).
  • 仍汲粪汁洒之，复成人，亦十余度 - Demons sprinkle excrement and restore the tortured to their original form, a cyclical process.
  • 余问‘此治何事？’黄巾曰：‘此世之小人，谤毁君子者，治于此。’ - Dialogue between the narrator and Huangjin, explaining the purpose of the hellish realm.
  • 不须遍历，直引去那里看了罢！ - Suggestion to visit another hellish realm without going through all of them.
  • 惩戒赃滥 - Punishment of corruption and embezzlement, emphasizing moral and ethical dimensions.
  • 夜叉数辈，状貌狞恶 - Demonic figures described as 'ferocious and terrifying' (状貌狞恶).
  • 以铁索牵八九饿鬼来，夜叉抽刀于裸者胸股间割肉，置锅中煎之，以啖饿鬼 - Demons drag and cut the condemned using 'iron chains' (铁索) and 'knives' (刀).
  • 啖尽又割，至余筋骨而后已 - Relentless and cruel nature of the punishment, cutting until only bones remain.
  • 少焉，业风一吹，肢体如故 - Karmic wind restores the tortured to their original state.
  • 皆人间清要之官，而招权纳赂，欺世盗名，或于任所阳为廉洁，而阴受包苴，或于乡里恃其官势，而吩咐公事，凡瞒人利己之徒，皆在其中 - High-ranking officials described as corrupt and unethical, emphasizing their positions of power and influence.

  • 黄巾猛一推余，不觉跌入尸内，恍然而寤矣 - The yellow-hatted person pushed me fiercely, I didn't realize I fell into the body, suddenly woke up; suggests a supernatural or dreamlike experience.

  • 两川都辖院志 - Records of the Two Rivers Prefecture; title of the text.
  • 京口吉复卿，唐吉温之后 - Ji Fucheng of Jingkou, descendant of Tang Jiwen; provides background information about the main character.
  • 宋建炎间，有讳深者，补润之金坛尉，遂尔家焉 - During the Jianyan period of the Song dynasty, there was a man named Shen, he was appointed as the military governor of Jintan, and thus settled there; gives historical context and the character's background.
  • 子孙世为金坛人，以资雄乡邑 - His descendants have lived in Jintan for generations, they have been wealthy and influential in their hometown; describes the family's status.

  • 复卿气豪，勇于为义 - Fucheng was bold and generous, he was brave in doing what was right; describes the character's personality.

  • 得夫、彦益与昵甚厚，复卿屡劝止之，往来自若 - Zhao Defu and Jiang Yanyi were very close to them, Fucheng repeatedly tried to stop them, but they continued as before; describes the character's attempts to influence others.

  • 瀹茗入诗瓢 - Brewing tea and using a gourd to drink it; symbol of simplicity and naturalness, reflecting a Zen-like appreciation of life.
  • 玉貌楼前堕 - Beautiful appearance falling before the tower; metaphor for the transience of beauty and the impermanence of life.
  • 冰容梦里消 - Cold demeanor disappearing in a dream; suggests the fleeting nature of emotions and the ephemeral quality of dreams.
  • 芳园曾藉坐 - Using a fragrant garden as a seat; metaphor for finding comfort and solace in nature.
  • 长路或追镳 - Chasing the reins on a long road; metaphor for the pursuit of goals or ideals.
  • 罗扇姬藏瓣 - The lady hiding the petals of a flower; metaphor for the concealment of beauty or secrets.
  • 筠篱仆护苗 - The servant guarding young plants; metaphor for the care and protection of something valuable.
  • 折来随手尽 - Picking and finishing with a casual gesture; metaphor for the ease and naturalness of certain actions.
  • 带处近环焦 - The belt near the ring of fire; metaphor for the proximity of danger or conflict.
  • 泥涴犹凄惨 - Muddy and sad condition; metaphor for the hardships and struggles of life.
  • 瓶空更寂寥 - Empty bottle being even more lonely; metaphor for the emptiness and loneliness that comes with loss.
  • 叶浓阴自厚 - Dense leaves creating a thick shade; metaphor for the protective and nurturing qualities of nature.
  • 蒂密子偏饶 - Dense stems with plentiful seeds; metaphor for abundance and fertility.
  • 岂必分茵溷 - Question of why one should necessarily divide the mat and the toilet; metaphor for the unnecessary distinctions between high and low.
  • 宁思上砑硝 - Preference for grinding nitre; metaphor for the pursuit of higher goals or ideals.
  • 香余何吝窃 - Question of why one should be stingy and steal the fragrant remainder; metaphor for the generosity and sharing of resources.
  • 佩解不烦邀 - Unfastening the pendant without bothering to invite; metaphor for the simplicity and ease of certain actions.
  • 冶态宜宫额 - Seductive appearance suitable for the palace forehead; metaphor for the beauty and grace that are appropriate for high places.
  • 痴情妒舞腰 - Foolish affection jealous of the dancer's waist; metaphor for the intensity and complexity of human emotions.
  • 妆台休浪拂 - Not casually wiping the dressing table; metaphor for the careful and deliberate nature of certain actions.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 但诸郎夜来谈笑，以长安娼女、浮梁商妇见目，毋亦太过乎 - But the young men talked and laughed last night, referring to a prostitute from Chang'an and a merchant's wife from Fuliang, isn't that a bit excessive?

  • 朗忆之乎？即昨日所讴之《念奴娇》也 - Do you remember it? It is the *Nian Nu Jiao* that I sang yesterday.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 离离禾黍 - Poetic expression meaning 'dense and lush crops,' often used to evoke a sense of pastoral beauty or nostalgia.
  • 叹江山似旧 - Means 'to lament that the landscape seems unchanged,' suggesting a contrast between the enduring natural world and the fleeting nature of human affairs.
  • 英雄尘土 - Literally 'heroes turned to dust,' a metaphor for the transience of human glory and the inevitability of death.
  • 石马铜驼荆棘里 - Refers to stone horses and bronze camels buried in thorns, symbolizing the decay of grandeur and the passage of time.
  • 剑戟灰飞，旌旗鸟散 - Metaphors for the destruction of military might and the dispersal of armies.
  • 底处寻楼橹 - Means 'where to find the watchtowers,' symbolizing the search for remnants of past glory.
  • 喑呜叱吒 - Describes the sound of a hero's roar, evoking the power and presence of a warrior.
  • 憔悴玉帐虞兮 - Refers to a withered and weary general, likely alluding to Xiang Yu (西楚霸王), a famous hero of the Chu-Han Contention.
  • 灯前掩面，泪交飞红雨 - A vivid description of someone weeping bitterly, with tears falling like a red rain.
  • 凤辇羊车行不返 - Refers to a phoenix carriage and a sheep-drawn cart, both symbols of imperial splendor, suggesting a journey that does not return.
  • 九曲愁肠慢苦 - Describes a heart full of sorrow and pain, twisted and coiled like a winding river.
  • 梅瓣凝妆，杨花飞雪 - Metaphors for delicate and fleeting beauty, with plum blossoms and willow catkins symbolizing the ephemeral nature of life.
  • 翠螺青黛，绛仙慵画眉妩 - Describes a beautiful woman with her eyebrows drawn in a lazy, yet alluring manner, using '翠螺' (green snail) and '青黛' (blue-black pigment) to evoke the elegance of her appearance.

  • 奋攘螳臂，拒抗鹰扬 - Struggling like a mantis against a chariot, resisting like an eagle spreading its wings.
  • 豕殪蛇殂，大将已歼于湖水 - Pigs and snakes perished, great generals were killed in the lake.
  • 鲸诛鲵戮，幻身旋毙于箭锋 - Killing whales and dolphins, phantom bodies quickly die at the arrow tips.
  • 一败天亡，六军星散 - One defeat leads to heavenly ruin, the six armies disperse like stars.
  • 特五大王一人而已 - Only the Fifth Prince alone.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 元末群雄起灭事 - Refers to the political turmoil and power struggles during the late Yuan Dynasty, including the rise and fall of various leaders and factions.
  • 《春秋》为尊者讳，为亲者讳 - A principle from the *Spring and Autumn Annals* that emphasizes the importance of respecting authority and family ties.
  • 陈主 - Refers to Chen Youliang, a prominent figure during the late Yuan Dynasty who founded the Chen Dynasty.
  • 斗筲小人 - A term used to describe individuals of low moral character, often associated with corruption and incompetence.
  • 秉钧轴，握兵符 - Terms describing positions of power and authority, such as holding the balance of power and commanding the military.
  • 在散地，处闲官 - Describes the status of officials who are in remote or insignificant positions and hold idle official posts.
  • 武弁则纵情酒色，文吏则惟事空言 - Criticizes the behavior of military officers and civil officials, highlighting the former's indulgence in vice and the latter's empty talk.
  • 城门狭而弗能容辇，爰作飞桥 - Describes a practical solution to a problem, specifically the construction of a flying bridge to overcome the narrow city gates.
  • 九江陋而锐于建都，犹余故址 - Describes the state of Jiujiang, emphasizing its crude nature but also its ambition to establish a capital.
  • 潜弑寿辉，显居厥位 - Refers to a treacherous act of secretly assassinating Shouhui and openly occupying his position.
  • 改元建号，弟兄井底之子阳 - Describes the act of changing the era name and establishing a new title, using a metaphor to describe internal strife.
  • 狭量浅谋，奴仆江南之李景 - Criticizes the lack of vision and strategy, comparing the situation to that of Li Jing, a historical figure.
  • 奋攘螳臂，拒抗鹰扬 - Uses metaphors to describe futile resistance.
  • 豕殪蛇殂，大将已歼于湖水 - Describes the defeat of the main forces, using vivid imagery.
  • 鲸诛鲵戮，幻身旋毙于箭锋 - Describes the swift and decisive nature of the defeat, using powerful metaphors.

  • 且谈风月，不必深言 - Suggests a desire to discuss light, pleasant topics rather than delve into serious or heavy matters. '风月' often refers to romantic or poetic themes.
  • 徒令人怀抱作恶耳 - Means that discussing serious matters would only make one feel uneasy or distressed. '怀抱作恶' is an idiomatic way of saying to feel bad or uncomfortable.
  • 因口占一诗曰 - Indicates that the speaker composed a poem spontaneously, often in a casual or informal setting. '口占' means to compose poetry orally.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 韶是晚再去 - 韶 likely refers to a person, possibly a historical figure or a character in a story. 是晚 means that evening, and 去 means to go again or to return.
  • 金雁已先在矣 - 金雁 could be a person's name or a metaphorical term. 已先在矣 means was already there.
  • 遂导过亭北竹阴中 - 遂 means then or thereupon, 导 means to lead or guide, 亭北 means north of the pavilion, and 竹阴中 means under the shade of bamboo.
  • 半里余 - 半里 means half a li (a traditional Chinese unit of distance), and 余 means more than or about.
  • 见朱门素壁，灯烛交辉 - 朱门 means red gate or grand entrance, 素壁 means plain walls, and 灯烛交辉 means lanterns and candles shining together.
  • 才及重堂，丽人迎笑 - 才及 means just reached, 重堂 means grand hall, and 丽人迎笑 means a beautiful woman greeted with a smile.
  • 出紫玉杯饮韶曰 - 出 means to take out, 紫玉杯 means purple jade cup, and 饮韶曰 means to drink with Shao and say.
  • 此吾主所御，今以劝郎，意亦不薄矣 - 此 means this, 吾主 means my master, 所御 means used by, 今以劝郎 means now to encourage you, and 意亦不薄矣 means the meaning is not shallow.
  • 留宿月余，不啻胶漆 - 留宿 means to stay overnight, 月余 means more than a month, and 不啻胶漆 means no different from glue and lacquer (indicating a very strong bond).
  • 一夕，丽人语韶曰 - 一夕 means one night, and 语韶曰 means told Shao.
  • 妾死时，伪汉方盛，主宠复深 - 妾 means I (used by a woman), 死时 means at the time of death, 伪汉 means false Han (likely referring to a usurper or a pretender), 方盛 means was flourishing, and 主宠复深 means the master's favor was deep.
  • 故玉匣珠襦，殡送极一时之富贵 - 故 means therefore, 玉匣 means jade box, 珠襦 means pearl robe, 殡送 means funeral procession, and 极一时之富贵 means reached the peak of wealth and honor at the time.
  • 幽宫神道，坟茔备一品之威仪 - 幽宫 means dark palace or tomb, 神道 means spirit road or tomb path, 坟茔 means tomb, and 备一品之威仪 means equipped with the dignity of a first-rank official.
  • 是故五体依然，三魂不昧 - 是故 means therefore, 五体 means five limbs (a metaphor for the whole body), 依然 means still, 三魂 means three souls, and 不昧 means not lost.
  • 向者庐君爱女南极夫人，偶此嬉游 - 向者 means formerly, 庐君 means Lord Lu, 爱女 means beloved daughter, 南极夫人 means Lady of the South Pole (a title), and 偶此嬉游 means happened to play here.
  • 授妾以太阴炼形之术 - 授 means to teach, 妾 means I (used by a woman), 以 means with, 太阴 means Great Yin (a term in Taoist alchemy), 炼形 means to refine the form, and 之术 means the technique.
  • 为之既久，不异生人 - 为之 means doing it, 既久 means for a long time, and 不异生人 means no different from a living person.
  • 夜出昼藏，逍遥自在 - 夜出 means going out at night, 昼藏 means hiding during the day, and 逍遥自在 means free and easy.
  • 君宜就市求青羊乳半杯 - 君 means you, 宜 means should, 就市 means to the market, 求 means to seek, 青羊乳 means green sheep's milk, and 半杯 means half a cup.
  • 勤勤滴妾目中 - 勤勤 means frequently or diligently, 滴 means to drip, and 妾目中 means into my eyes.
  • 乳尽眼开，白日可起 - 乳尽 means the milk is finished, 眼开 means eyes open, and 白日可起 means can rise during the day.
  • 韶如言求得，以润其两眦 - 韶如言 means Shao as said, 求得 means obtained, 以 means to, 润 means to moisten, and 其两眦 means his two corners of the eyes.
  • 屈指三旬，然能步 - 屈指 means to count on one's fingers, 三旬 means three weeks, 然 means then, and 能步 means can walk.
  • 或同携素手，游行隧中 - 或 means sometimes, 同携 means together carry, 素手 means white hands (a poetic way of saying hands), 游行 means to walk, and 隧中 means in the tunnel.
  • 或并倚香肩，笑歌亭上 - 或 means sometimes, 并倚 means to lean together, 香肩 means fragrant shoulders (a poetic way of saying shoulders), 笑歌 means laughing and singing, and 亭上 means on the pavilion.
  • 与韶论旧事曰 - 与 means with, 韶 means Shao, 论 means to discuss, 旧事 means old matters, and 曰 means said.
  • 未及十二三年，便成陈迹 - 未及 means not even, 十二三年 means twelve or thirteen years, and 便成陈迹 means become old memories.
  • 吾主一日读《天宝遗事》而喜之 - 吾主 means my master, 一日 means one day, 读 means to read, 《天宝遗事》 means The Lost Tales of Tianbao (a historical text), and 而喜之 means and found it pleasing.
  • 故春秋宫中设宴，令妾辈竞簪奇花 - 故 means therefore, 春秋 means Spring and Autumn (referring to the period or the seasons), 宫中 means in the palace, 设宴 means to hold a banquet, 令 means to order, 妾辈 means we women, 竞 means to compete, 簪 means to wear (flowers in the hair), and 奇花 means exotic flowers.
  • 蝶闻花馥，飞著钗端 - 蝶 means butterfly, 闻 means to smell, 花馥 means fragrance of flowers, 飞著 means flying and landing, and 钗端 means the tip of the hairpin.
  • 所止之人，是夕得召，谓之蝶幸 - 所止 means where it lands, 之人 means that person, 是夕 means that night, 得召 means was summoned, and 谓之蝶幸 means called it a butterfly's favor.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 至正戊戌 - The year 1348 during the Yuan dynasty.
  • 田丰破东平 - Tian Feng, a rebel leader, conquered Dongping.
  • 毛贵复陷东昌 - Mao Gui, another rebel leader, reconquered Dongzhou.
  • 伪将俞左丞 - A pretend commander named Yu, who was actually a loyalist.
  • 赵氏 - Zhao, the wife of the narrator, who is being searched for.
  • 周万户 - A proper name, possibly a high-ranking official or military leader.
  • 监者 - Guards or jailers, who would enforce the rules of the prison.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '明年，至正戊戌，田丰破东平，颖与鸾相失，莫知所在' is a sequence of events, with '明年' setting the time frame, '至正戊戌' specifying the year, and the subsequent clauses describing the actions and their consequences.
  • The clause '毛贵复陷东昌，留伪将俞左丞者镇守' describes the actions of Mao Gui and the appointment of a pretend commander.
  • The clause '俞颇知道理，凡所掠男女，出榜召人识认给还' explains the actions of Yu, the pretend commander, and his understanding of righteousness.
  • The clause '颖闻之，意鸾或者在彼，冲冒白刃中，求而未得' describes the narrator's reaction and his efforts to find Zhao.
  • The clause '正忧窘间，有指女冠院语之曰：“盍不于此访求乎？”' describes the narrator's state of anxiety and the suggestion to search for Zhao in the nunnery.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 颖 - Refers to Ying, the protagonist who is actively involved in the story.
  • 万户 - Refers to a high-ranking military official, often used in historical contexts to denote a powerful figure.
  • 鸾 - Refers to Lu, the wife of Ying, who is being held captive.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 颖得书，则又间关跋涉，达于彼中 - This sentence describes Ying receiving a letter and then traveling arduously to reach the place where Lu is held.
  • 万户方拥重兵，赫然声势，未敢轻进，投其邻而安下焉 - This sentence describes the powerful official with a large army, who is cautious and settles near his neighbor.
  • 越数日，缉知鸾之在也，而无由以通消息，乃日伺于门 - This sentence describes the passage of several days, the discovery of Lu's location, and the persistent waiting by the door.
  • 见一巫媪，往来频数，意必府中之亲信人也 - This sentence describes the observation of an elderly woman who frequently visits, indicating she is a trusted person in the household.
  • 候媪出，潜随至家，奉银一锭为寿，而以情告焉 - This sentence describes waiting for the old woman to leave, following her home, presenting a gift, and telling the situation.
  • 夫人曰：“吾无所用，况其夫在，何忍留之？当即遣还。” - This sentence describes the lady's decision to release Lu due to her husband's presence.
  • 夫妇相携拜辞而出 - This sentence describes the couple's respectful departure.

Proper Nouns

  • 颖 - Ying, the protagonist.
  • 万户 - A high-ranking military official.
  • 鸾 - Lu, the wife of Ying.
  • 巫媪 - An elderly woman who practices witchcraft or divination.
  • 夫人 - The wife of the high-ranking official.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 我生之初尚无为 - At the beginning of my life, there was still no action. Reflects a state of innocence or simplicity before the onset of hardship.
  • 我生之后元运衰 - After my birth, the great fortune declined. Suggests a decline in fortune or prosperity following the speaker's birth.
  • 夫与妻兮忽仳离 - Husband and wife, suddenly separated. Expresses the sudden and unexpected separation of a married couple.
  • 父与母兮生死安可知 - Father and mother, who knows if they are alive or dead? Conveys the uncertainty and despair of not knowing the fate of one's parents.
  • 狼烟四起兮沸鼓鼙 - Wolf smoke rises all around, drums and gongs resound. Describes the chaos and turmoil of war.
  • 锋镝成林兮盛旌旗 - Spears and arrows form a forest, flags are abundant. Vividly depicts the intensity of battle.
  • 人民涂炭兮城郭坏 - The people suffer, cities are destroyed. Highlights the devastation and suffering of the populace.
  • 礼义灭亡兮法度隳 - Rituals and righteousness are lost, laws are destroyed. Indicates the collapse of social order and moral values.
  • 身流落兮天一涯 - My body is lost, far away in the world. Expresses the feeling of being far from home and in a state of exile.
  • 肠欲绝兮心孔悲 - My intestines are about to tear, my heart is deeply sorrowful. Conveys intense emotional pain.
  • 山可平兮河可塞 - Mountains can be leveled, rivers can be blocked. A hyperbolic expression of extreme suffering.
  • 妾怨苦兮无穷期 - My grievances and sorrows are endless. Expresses the speaker's endless suffering and resentment.
  • 蜂蚁屯聚兮豺虎嗥 - Bees and ants gather, tigers and wolves howl. Describes the chaotic and dangerous situation.
  • 心毒狠兮体腥臊 - The heart is cruel, the body is foul-smelling. Describes the brutality and corruption of the times.
  • 烟尘澒洞兮人窜逃 - Smoke and dust fill the air, people flee. Depicts the chaos and panic caused by war.
  • 寒沙暴骨兮没蓬蒿 - Cold sand is covered with bones, buried in weeds. Describes the desolate and tragic aftermath of war.
  • 亡家遇乱兮伤吾曹 - Losing our homes in chaos, we are deeply hurt. Expresses the collective suffering of the people.
  • 义重命轻兮如鸿毛 - Duty is heavy, life is light, like a feather. Emphasizes the importance of duty over personal safety.
  • 誓捐此生兮期不污 - I swear to dedicate my life, hoping not to be tainted. Expresses a vow to remain pure and honorable.
  • 仰天俯地兮独烦劳 - Looking up to the sky and down to the earth, I am alone and troubled. Conveys a sense of isolation and inner turmoil.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The couplet '我生之初尚无为，我生之后元运衰' contrasts the speaker's early life with the subsequent decline in fortune.

  • The couplet '人民涂炭兮城郭坏，礼义灭亡兮法度隳' highlights the destruction of the people and the collapse of social order.
  • The couplet '身流落兮天一涯，肠欲绝兮心孔悲' expresses the speaker's feelings of being far from home and the intense emotional pain.
  • The couplet '山可平兮河可塞，妾怨苦兮无穷期' uses hyperbole to express the speaker's endless suffering and resentment.
  • The couplet '蜂蚁屯聚兮豺虎嗥，心毒狠兮体腥臊' describes the chaotic and dangerous situation and the brutality of the times.
  • The couplet '烟尘澒洞兮人窜逃，寒沙暴骨兮没蓬蒿' depicts the chaos and panic caused by war and the desolate aftermath.
  • The couplet '亡家遇乱兮伤吾曹，义重命轻兮如鸿毛' expresses the collective suffering of the people and the importance of duty over personal safety.
  • The couplet '誓捐此生兮期不污，仰天俯地兮独烦劳' expresses a vow to remain pure and honorable and the sense of isolation and inner turmoil.

  • 鸾幽女妇，乃能乱离中全节不污 - Means 'Luan, an ordinary woman, could maintain her chastity in times of chaos,' where '幽女妇' (ordinary woman) and '全节不污' (maintain her chastity) emphasize her virtue.

  • 世之抱琵琶过别船者，闻鸾之风，其真可愧哉 - Means 'those who hold the pipa and pass by other boats, hearing of Luan's spirit, truly feel ashamed,' where '抱琵琶过别船者' (those who hold the pipa and pass by other boats) is a metaphor for those who abandon their principles for personal gain.

  • 倘因缘会合，得为夫妇，虽死无憾 - Phrase meaning 'if fate brings us together and we can be husband and wife, I will have no regrets even if I die,' indicating the daughter's strong desire for the marriage.
  • 不然，我之嫁人，非商家郎，则耕家子 - Phrase meaning 'otherwise, I will only marry a merchant's son or a farmer's son,' indicating the daughter's preference for certain types of husbands.
  • 纵金玉满堂，田连阡陌，不愿也 - Phrase meaning 'even if the house is filled with gold and jade, and the fields stretch far and wide, I would not want it,' indicating the daughter's disdain for material wealth.
  • 得子为配，足慰平生 - Phrase meaning 'if I can have you as my partner, it will be enough to satisfy my life,' indicating the son-in-law's satisfaction with the prospect of marriage.
  • 若余事成，开花结子；事若不成，根枯叶死 - Phrase meaning 'if the matter succeeds, the plant will bloom and bear fruit; if it fails, the roots will wither and the leaves will die,' indicating a metaphorical oath.
  • 誓毕，散去 - Phrase meaning 'after the oath, they parted,' indicating the end of their meeting.
  • 生盘桓祖氏，大小悦之 - Phrase meaning 'the son-in-law stayed at the grandmother's house, and everyone was pleased with him,' indicating the son-in-law's popularity.
  • 女尤敬慕 - Phrase meaning 'the daughter especially admired him,' indicating the daughter's high regard for the son-in-law.
  • 尝亲奉茶与生 - Phrase meaning 'she once personally served tea to the son-in-law,' indicating the daughter's respectful gesture.
  • 茶已吃矣，不患不成 - Phrase meaning 'the tea has been drunk, so there is no worry that the matter will not succeed,' indicating the son-in-law's confidence in the marriage.
  • 家人闻之，亦不问也 - Phrase meaning 'the family heard about it but did not question it,' indicating the family's acceptance of the situation.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '僧曰：“诸公久得禅悦，当避机锋，然文士在席，何不且辍空谈，更裁佳句，以为清宵欢乐之资乎？”' is a complex rhetorical question posed by the monk, suggesting a shift from spiritual practice to literary creation.
  • The brief response '众曰：“诺！”' indicates agreement but lacks further elaboration.
  • The phrase '子见先吟曰' introduces the first poem, but the identity of '子见' is not specified, leaving some ambiguity about who is speaking.

Idiomatic Expressions

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 近亦被人劈而为薪 - '劈而为薪' means 'split and used as firewood.'
  • 止余盖在 - '止余' means 'only remaining,' and '盖' refers to the lid or top of the coffin.
  • 子所遇石子见、毛原颖，非砚与笔乎 - '石子见' and '毛原颖' are proper names, possibly referring to stones and animal hair, which are used to make inkstones and brushes, respectively.
  • 金兆祥、曾瓦合，非铫与甑乎 - '金兆祥' and '曾瓦合' are proper names, possibly referring to metal and clay, which are used to make cooking utensils like '铫' (a type of pot) and '甑' (a steamer).
  • 皮以礼则被字，木如愚则木鱼 - '皮以礼则被字' means 'leather, when treated with respect, is inscribed with characters,' and '木如愚则木鱼' means 'wood, when treated as if it were foolish, becomes a wooden fish (a prayer tool).'
  • 上官盖为棺材，罗本素乃旧扇 - '上官盖为棺材' means 'the official's cover became a coffin,' and '罗本素乃旧扇' means 'the plain cloth became an old fan.'
  • 即子所见数物颠倒为惑也 - '颠倒为惑' means 'became confused or disordered.'
  • 其曰有与项氏亲戚者，盖指棺而言耳 - '亲戚' means 'relative,' and '盖指棺而言耳' means 'refers to the coffin.'
  • 棺为项氏故物，故曰亲戚也 - '故物' means 'old thing' or 'former possession.'
  • 仲和默然，惴栗特甚 - '惴栗特甚' means 'extremely nervous and fearful.'
  • 即日回家，果得重病 - '果得重病' means 'indeed fell seriously ill.'

  • 新郑北门老狐精 - Refers to an old fox spirit from the northern gate of Xinzheng (新郑), a place name in Henan Province. In Chinese folklore, fox spirits (狐精) are often depicted as shape-shifters who can transform into humans, particularly women, and are known for their seductive powers.
  • 萧裕 - The name of a state official (州判), likely a real historical figure, though no specific historical record is provided in the text. The reference to him as '萧君' suggests he is a respected figure.
  • 邓、辛、张三帅 - These are likely references to legendary Daoist generals or deities, such as General Deng (邓将军), General Xin (辛将军), and General Zhang (张将军), who are often invoked in exorcism rituals.
  • 檄 - A formal written order or proclamation, often used in military or ritual contexts. Here, it refers to a document addressed to the gods or spirits, commanding them to act.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 二氣始判，而天高地下，自此奠其儀 - Cosmological statement: When the two primary forces (yin and yang) first differentiated, heaven became high and earth low, thus establishing their order.
  • 三才已分，而物化人生，亦各從其類 - Statement on cosmic order: When the Three Talents (heaven, earth, and humanity) were distinguished, all things and human life followed their respective categories.
  • 念幅員之既廣，慨狐魅之滋多 - Statement of lament: Considering the vastness of the territory, one laments the proliferation of foxes and demons.
  • 擊尾出火以作祟，聽冰渡水而致疑 - Describes supernatural actions: They strike their tails to produce fire and cause mischief, and listen to ice crossing water to create suspicion.
  • 百丈破因果之禪，大安入羅漢之地 - Refers to historical figures or events: Master Baizhang broke the cycle of karma in his Zen teachings, and Dashi entered the realm of Arhats.
  • 再思多佞，難逃兩腳之譏 - Proverbial statement: Reishin, who was overly flattering, could not escape ridicule from his two legs.
  • 司空博聞，能識千年之怪 - Proverbial statement: Sikong, with his broad knowledge, could recognize strange phenomena from a thousand years ago.
  • 況蕭裕乃八閩進士，七品命官，而敢薦爾腥臊，奪其精氣 - Statement of accusation: Moreover, Xiao Yu, a scholar-official from Fujian with a seventh-rank appointment, dared to recommend you, a stinking demon, to steal their vital energy.
  • 恣烏合而弗慚，懷豕心而未已 - Describes immoral behavior: They freely gathered without shame, harboring the heart of a pig and not ceasing.
  • 過可文乎？言之丑也！ - Rhetorical question: Can such actions be justified? They are indeed shameful!
  • 郡城隍失於覺察，權且姑容 - Statement of leniency: The city god failed to detect this, and so we temporarily tolerate it.
  • 衙土地乃爾隱藏，另行究治 - Statement of punishment: The local land deities hid you, and so we will investigate and punish them separately.
  • 其青丘之正犯，論黑簿之嚴刑 - Describes punishment: The chief culprit from Qingxiu will be judged by the strict laws of the Black Register.
  • 押赴市曹，斃於雷斧 - Describes execution: They will be dragged to the marketplace and killed by the thunderbolt axe.
  • 使虎威之莫假，庶兔悲而有懲 - Statement of deterrence: To ensure that the tiger's power is not abused, we hope to deter even the timid rabbit.
  • 九尾盡誅，萬劫不赦 - Statement of severe punishment: All nine-tailed foxes will be exterminated, and they will not be forgiven for a thousand kalpas.
  • 耀州衙速令清淨，新鄭驛永絕根苗 - Statement of purification: The office of Ya Zhou must be quickly cleansed, and the New Zheng Post Station must be forever rid of its roots.
  • 長閉鬼門之關，一准酆都之律 - Statement of final judgment: They will be permanently closed off at the gate of Ghost City, following the laws of Fengdu.
  • 布告廟社，咸使風聞 - Statement of public announcement: This will be announced in temples and shrines, so that all may hear of it.
